title: External source evidence — Anthropic's published agent-building guidance
what: The exact external publications this advisory reference draws on, with canonical URL, publisher, retrieval date, and the minimal claims used. A URL is provenance metadata (a locator), NOT a terminal authority anchor; the taxonomy contract pins THIS file by its git blob sha (file:@<sha>).
why: Authority-anchored discipline: taxonomy claims must terminate in a reviewable, content-addressed evidence artifact rather than a bare URL or a retrieval date.
authority: git:9d322ddb9c7d679414b3822c3d8128575ddb3a3e (this cycle) + The Eye ruling: pre-review accepted 2026-07-17
non_claim: This evidence does not reproduce the publications in full (licensing); it preserves the minimal claims used and their provenance. It does not assert Anthropic endorses TELOS or its worked examples.
change_rule: Adding/altering a source is a reviewed edit to this file; the taxonomy's file:@<sha> pin must be updated to the new blob sha or the local checker fails closed.

sources.title: Building Effective Agents
claims_used: The augmented LLM (model + retrieval + tools + memory) is the basic building block.
Five workflow patterns are distinguished from autonomous agents: prompt chaining, routing, parallelization, orchestrator-workers, evaluator-optimizer.
Workflows orchestrate LLMs/tools through predefined code paths; agents dynamically direct their own process and tool use.
Simplicity first: find the simplest solution and increase complexity only when it demonstrably improves outcomes; agentic systems trade latency/cost/compounding-error risk for capability.
Three agent design principles: simplicity, transparency (show planning steps), a well-crafted agent-computer interface.
Autonomous agents need ground-truth checkpoints (tests, human review).

sources.title: Writing tools for AI agents
claims_used: Prefer a few high-impact consolidated tools over one-per-endpoint.
Namespace related tools; return meaningful human-readable context; optimize for token efficiency; write clear descriptions; evaluate on real tasks measuring runtime + tokens.

sources.title: Claude Agent SDK overview
claims_used: The SDK packages the agent loop, context management (compaction), permissions (allowed/disallowed tools), hooks (PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop/...), subagents, and MCP.

sources.title: Effective context engineering for AI agents
claims_used: Manage the context window deliberately (compaction/summarization of older turns).

sources.title: How we built our multi-agent research system
claims_used: An orchestrator-workers multi-agent system with a lead agent delegating to subagents is effective for broad, parallelizable research.

sources.title: Effective harnesses for long-running agents
claims_used: Long-running agents need harnesses that let them resume, verify, and stay on-budget rather than drift.
